title: ED pelvic pain documentation

red_flags.text: Lists pain, amenorrhoea, bleeding, gastrointestinal symptoms, dizziness or syncope, shoulder-tip pain, urinary symptoms, tenderness, haemodynamic signs, pregnancy testing, and escalation criteria.
red_flags.rationale: When pregnancy is possible or known, the exact NICE section supports recording vital data, time-critical symptoms and clinician-decided escalation without asserting ectopic pregnancy or miscarriage.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists pain, amenorrhoea, bleeding, gastrointestinal symptoms, dizziness or syncope, shoulder-tip pain, urinary symptoms, tenderness, haemodynamic signs, pregnancy testing, and escalation criteria.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management

examination.text: Specifies pain pattern, menstrual, bladder, bowel, movement, posture, function and medication history plus abdominal and pelvic examination for focal tenderness or structural findings.
examination.rationale: The exact RCOG chronic pelvic-pain history and examination section supports onset, duration, severity, impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, patient concerns, risk context and manual focused examination in women with chronic pain; it does not cover all acute ED pelvic pain.
examination.evidence_extract: Specifies pain pattern, menstrual, bladder, bowel, movement, posture, function and medication history plus abdominal and pelvic examination for focal tenderness or structural findings.

source.title: The Initial Management of Chronic Pelvic Pain — Green-top Guideline No. 41

investigations.text: Defines clinician-performed transvaginal or selected transabdominal ultrasound and cautions against overinterpreting one early scan or LMP alone.
investigations.rationale: When early pregnancy applies, the exact ultrasound section supports recording imaging or test results already performed or reviewed, without recommending or interpreting them.
investigations.evidence_extract: Defines clinician-performed transvaginal or selected transabdominal ultrasound and cautions against overinterpreting one early scan or LMP alone.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.7.6 — ultrasound for pregnancy location and viability

investigations.text: Supports clinician-selected STI sampling when PID is suspected and transvaginal ultrasound for adnexal masses and selected chronic pelvic-pain pathology.
investigations.rationale: Investigation recommendations were reviewed but not mapped to automatic tests.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-selected STI sampling when PID is suspected and transvaginal ultrasound for adnexal masses and selected chronic pelvic-pain pathology.

source.title: The Initial Management of Chronic Pelvic Pain — Green-top Guideline No. 41

exact_section.heading: 6.1 Screening for Infection; 6.2 Transvaginal Scanning and MRI

treatment_and_management.text: Defines chronic pelvic pain as a symptom rather than a diagnosis and frames initial assessment around the woman’s concerns, function, and multifactorial context.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact initial-assessment section supports a clinician-entered plan, completed discussion and arranged follow-up only when documented; it does not generate management.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Defines chronic pelvic pain as a symptom rather than a diagnosis and frames initial assessment around the woman’s concerns, function, and multifactorial context.

source.title: The Initial Management of Chronic Pelvic Pain — Green-top Guideline No. 41

exact_section.heading: 1. Purpose and Scope; 2. Background; 5. Initial Assessment

source_limitations: Acute non-pregnancy causes, male pelvic pain, detailed menstrual/sexual/urinary/bowel history, examination appropriateness, tests, diagnosis, treatment and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
